rationale: The citing paper uses Manchanda et al. (2006) in the own-channel literature review with a specific empirical takeaway, noting its estimate of online display effects on repurchase behavior (elasticity around .02). This is not just a name check; it helps establish the prior evidence base for display advertising effectiveness on purchasing outcomes. The citing paper then positions its contribution as going beyond that prior own-channel evidence to a multichannel, cross-channel setting.
relation_description: Dinner et al. treat Manchanda et al. (2006) as prior empirical grounding that banner/display exposure can influence online purchasing. They use this baseline to motivate a broader model of advertising effects across media and across online and offline sales channels.
